summary.intent: Address five Codex review thread findings on PR #724 in fogodev/ars-ui, push the fixes, resolve the threads, and trigger a fresh Codex review pass.
summary.outcome: All five original threads were fixed, replied to, and resolved. A follow-up Codex pass surfaced a Form status-region gap, which was implemented in both Leptos and Dioxus adapters along with CI formatting fixes, and committed as a second batch.

repo: The repo uses `cargo xtask ci fmt` as the CI formatting gate, which runs both `leptosfmt` and nightly rustfmt; `leptosfmt` failures silently cause downstream CI jobs to be skipped rather than explicitly fail.
Leptos and Dioxus adapters must be kept in parity for component behavior (e.g., StatusRegion, Field input_attrs, form submission lifecycle).
The `snapshot-reviewed` label must be reapplied after any snapshot-changing push to keep CI green.
The agnostic core (`ars-components`) emits `aria-disabled` only; native disabled is added by the concrete Leptos/Dioxus Input adapters — this is an intentional API boundary.
Form StatusRegion is spec'd to derive content from `api.status_message()` in both adapter specs (`spec/leptos-components/utility/form.md`, `spec/dioxus-components/utility/form.md`).
`dx fmt` normalizes Dioxus wasm test snippets as a side effect of formatting runs.

code.finding: StatusRegion renders live machine status via `api.status_message()`; children are preserved as adjacent/fallback content.
Dioxus StatusRegion mirrors the Leptos change, reading live machine status message into the persistent live region.
Core form machine exposes `status_message()` and `status_region_attrs()` but does not yet expose a public status-message setter; render-path coverage is possible but setter-driven tests are deferred.

workflow: Run `leptosfmt --check --experimental-tailwind` directly (without quiet mode) to see the exact diff when `cargo xtask ci fmt` fails silently.

Use `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{number}/labels -f 'labels[]=label-name'` (with the key quoted) instead of `gh pr edit --add-label` when GitHub connection errors occur; bracket expansion in zsh requires the key to be quoted.
Batch all review fixes into a single commit before pushing to avoid multiple CI runs and reduce round-trips with the Codex reviewer.
Poll `gh pr checks` and `gh api .../reactions` together after triggering a Codex review to track both CI and review status in one pass.

friction: The commit hook ran silently for an extended period, requiring repeated polling before the push could proceed.
`gh pr edit --add-label snapshot-reviewed` failed with a transient GitHub connection error and had to be retried via the direct API endpoint.
zsh bracket expansion broke `gh api ... -f labels[]=snapshot-reviewed`; the key had to be quoted as `'labels[]=snapshot-reviewed'`.
`cargo xtask ci fmt` runs in quiet mode, hiding the actual leptosfmt diff and requiring a separate manual `leptosfmt --check` invocation to identify the exact lines to fix.
A fresh Codex review pass reopened a thread that duplicated an already-fixed issue (Leptos Field root invalid), adding noise to the review cycle.
open_items: No public setter for `status_message()` exists on the form machine yet; wasm tests for the status-region only cover the render path, not setter-driven announcements.

The duplicate Codex thread (Leptos Field root invalid) was identified as a false positive from the fresh review pass — it should be replied to and resolved in a subsequent step not shown in this transcript.
CI was still running at the end of the session; the outcome of the fresh Codex review pass and the full CI matrix for the status-region commit is unknown.
